Thought 4: The murder occurred during midday prayers. Gerald was confirmed to be at the mosque at that time via surveillance footage. Mark's alibi is vague; he only mentions a "standard law firm meeting" with no specific time mentioned, so it's unclear if he was at the mosque or not. However, Gerald's presence at the scene is established, while Mark's is not. Gerald also had a direct motive (fear and inferiority) and purchased a shotgun a week prior. Mark had a motive (fear of extremist ideas) and purchased shotgun shells, but the timing is unclear. The key point is that Gerald was at the scene, while Mark has no confirmed presence. Also, Gerald's shaky hands might be a red herring since he plays the violin, which requires steadiness, so the shakiness could be due to nervousness during the interview. Mark's knowledge of firearms and legal expertise might make him more careful, but Gerald's direct access and presence are stronger indicators. I think Gerald is the more likely murderer.

Action 4: Finish[1] (Gerald)
